name: Arrays
description: Linear contiguous data structure enabling indexed access and efficient iteration.

name: Linked Lists
description: Node-based sequential structure where elements are connected via pointers.

name: Stacks
description: LIFO (Last-In-First-Out) structure used for recursion, parsing, and backtracking.

name: Queues
description: FIFO (First-In-First-Out) structure commonly used in scheduling and traversal.

name: Trees
description: Hierarchical structure with parent-child relationships for efficient organization.

name: Graphs
description: Network structure consisting of vertices and edges modeling complex relationships.

name: Sorting Algorithms
description: Algorithms that arrange elements in a specific order based on comparison or rules.

name: Searching Algorithms
description: Algorithms designed to efficiently locate elements within datasets.

name: Dynamic Programming
description: Optimization technique solving problems via overlapping subproblems and memoization.

name: Greedy Algorithms
description: Strategy that builds solutions using locally optimal choices at each step.

name: Backtracking
description: Systematic exploration of solution spaces using recursion and pruning.

name: Sliding Window
description: Technique for processing subarrays or substrings using a moving window.

name: Two Pointers
description: Pattern using two indices to traverse data efficiently from different directions.

name: Prefix Sum
description: Precomputation technique enabling fast range-sum or cumulative queries.

name: Bit Manipulation
description: Low-level operations that manipulate binary representations of data.

name: Monotonic Stack
description: Stack structure maintaining sorted order for efficient range queries.

name: Top-K Elements
description: Techniques for efficiently identifying the largest or smallest K elements.

name: Recursion
description: Method where functions call themselves to break problems into subproblems.

name: Overlapping Intervals
description: Techniques for merging and analyzing overlapping interval ranges.

name: Tree Traversal
description: Methods for systematically visiting nodes in tree data structures.

name: Graph Traversal
description: Algorithms for exploring nodes and edges in graph structures.

name: Matrix Traversal
description: Strategies for iterating through two-dimensional grid structures.

name: Bubble Sort
description: Simple comparison sort that repeatedly swaps adjacent elements.

name: Selection Sort
description: Selects the minimum element and places it in sorted order.

name: Insertion Sort
description: Builds a sorted array by inserting elements one at a time.

name: Merge Sort
description: Divide-and-conquer sort with stable O(n log n) performance.

name: Quick Sort
description: Partition-based divide-and-conquer sorting algorithm.

name: Linear Search
description: Sequentially scans elements until a match is found.

name: Binary Search
description: Efficient search on sorted data using divide-and-conquer.

name: Fixed Window
description: Sliding window with constant size for range-based problems.

name: Variable Window
description: Dynamically adjusts window size based on constraints.

name: Preorder Traversal
description: Visits root before recursively traversing subtrees.

name: Inorder Traversal
description: Traverses left subtree, root, then right subtree.

name: Postorder Traversal
description: Traverses subtrees before visiting the root.

name: Level Order Traversal
description: Breadth-first traversal level by level.

name: Depth First Search
description: Explores graph deeply before backtracking.

name: Breadth First Search
description: Explores graph layer by layer using a queue.
